steps.title: The Witness Fails to Appear
steps.summary: Arthur Penhaligon, waiting in the hallway for the private midnight revelation Sir Alistair promised, grows concerned by the silence and enters the study to find the industrialist slumped over his desk.

steps.title: A Room Disturbed
steps.summary: Arthur secures the room and conducts a methodical sweep. He discovers a silver flask with an odd residue and a scrap of silk snagged on the corner of the heavy mahogany desk.
reveals: A silver brandy flask contains traces of Aconitine.
A green silk ribbon was left behind by someone in a hurry.

steps.title: The Household Gathers
steps.summary: Arthur informs the guests of the tragedy. He notes the reactions: Julian's sweating, Elspeth's icy detachment, and the Vances' clinical shock.
reveals: Julian Thorne was wearing a green costume that is now missing a ribbon.

steps.title: The Silent Library
steps.summary: Arthur checks Lady Elspeth's claim that she was reading in the library. He finds her wine glass with distinct lipstick marks, but the book she claimed to be reading is dust-covered and hasn't been moved in weeks.
reveals: Lady Elspeth's alibi is fabricated; she was not reading.

steps.title: The Weapon in the Ashes
steps.summary: A search of the study's hearth reveals a hunting knife wiped clean, but hidden hastily beneath the logs.
reveals: A decorative hunting knife was used to stab the victim.

steps.title: The Heir's Panic
steps.summary: Arthur confronts Julian about the green ribbon (EV-02) and the knife. Julian becomes aggressive and defensive, claiming he was on the terrace alone.
reveals: Julian Thorne has no witnesses for his whereabouts between 10:00 PM and 10:45 PM.

steps.title: The Muddy Terrace
steps.summary: Arthur examines the terrace. Despite Julian's claim of clearing his head, there are no footprints in the mud near the door, and the storm would have soaked anyone standing there in seconds.
reveals: Julian Thorne's alibi is physically impossible given the weather.

steps.title: The Motive of Debt
steps.summary: Arthur discovers a hidden ledger page in Julian's drawer. It details massive gambling debts that would provide a clear motive for murder to secure an immediate inheritance.
reveals: Julian Thorne was being hunted by debt collectors and needed his father's money urgently.

steps.title: The Bloodless Wound
steps.summary: Arthur re-examines the body with a keen eye. He notes the lack of blood spray from the knife wound, indicating Alistair's heart had already stopped when he was stabbed.
reveals: The stabbing was post-mortem; Julian's 'murder' was a panicked attempt to cover up a theft.

steps.title: The Doctor's Deception
steps.summary: As Arthur questions Dr. Vance, he notices the doctor attempting to shove a piece of paper into his medical bag. Arthur insists on seeing it, revealing a blackmail note.
reveals: Dr. Vance was being blackmailed by Alistair over a fatal surgical error.

steps.title: The Loyal Wife
steps.summary: Arthur speaks with Eleanor Vance. She is calm and supportive of her husband, insisting Percival was with her or in the washroom all evening, though her mask adjustments were conveniently unobserved.
reveals: Eleanor Vance was remarkably attentive to her husband's deteriorating mental state.

steps.title: The Gardener's Secret
steps.summary: Suspecting the poison's origin, Arthur visits the conservatory. He finds a discarded suede glove in the bin with crushed organic residue and soil.
reveals: Someone recently harvested monkshood from the manor's collection.

steps.title: The Beaded Handbag
steps.summary: While the guests are resting, Arthur checks the cloaks and bags in the hall. Inside Eleanor Vance's handbag, he finds a single, tell-tale purple petal.
reveals: Eleanor Vance was in possession of monkshood petals during the masquerade.

steps.title: The Midnight Reckoning
steps.summary: Arthur pieces together the timing: Julian arrived after the poison took effect, and Percival arrived after Julian, finding the body already stabbed. The revelation Alistair intended for midnight was the exposure of Percival, which Eleanor moved to prevent.
reveals: Eleanor Vance killed Alistair to protect her husband from the 'grand reckoning' Alistair planned for midnight.

steps.title: The Final Mask Removed
steps.summary: Arthur gathers the suspects in the study. He presents the silver flask (EV-01), the stained glove (EV-03), and the petal from the handbag (EV-07), systematically dismantling Eleanor's supportive facade.

steps.title: A Bitter Vintage
steps.summary: Eleanor Vance confesses, stating she would do it again to stop Alistair's cruelty from killing her husband's spirit. As the storm breaks and the police arrive at dawn, she is taken into custody while Julian and Elspeth are left to face the shame of their own smaller crimes.
reveals: The case is closed; the motive was protection, not greed.
